purpose: Conformance vectors for BasePay payment scenarios. Each vector models a pre-action claim + post-execution receipt pattern. Implementers integrating BasePay with action_ref must reproduce these action_refs byte-identical from the stated preimage fields.

vectors.authority_source: EIP-712 signature over (token, from, to, value, validAfter, validBefore, nonce)
vectors.description: Gasless EIP-3009 transfer. The pre-action claim carries transfer_mechanism, from, to, value, nonce, and valid_before. The relayer address MUST NOT appear in any claim field — the verifier checks relayer_not_in_claim == true.

invariants.description: The relayer address that submits the meta-transaction on-chain MUST NOT appear in from, to, or any other field of the pre-action claim. This preserves operator-independence: a verifier can confirm the claim without knowing which relayer was used.

The EIP-712 signature (v, r, s) over the TransferWithAuthorization struct MUST recover to claim.from. Verifier performs this check pre-submission without RPC — pure cryptographic check on the signed struct hash.
invariants.verify: ecrecover(eip712_hash(from, to, value, validAfter, validBefore, nonce), v, r, s) == claim.from
invariants.note: Off-chain verifier check only. The on-chain contract (USDC transferWithAuthorization) performs the same ecrecover — verifier replicates it to catch signer mismatches before broadcast.

invariants.description: block.timestamp at submission MUST be strictly less than validBefore. Verifier checks this against current time before broadcasting.
authorizationState(from, nonce) MUST be false at submission time. Verifier checks on-chain state to prevent replay.

vectors.authority_source: ERC-2612 permit signature + calldata (recipient set, amounts, memo)
vectors.description: Batch pay via ERC-2612 permit. The pre-action claim carries transfer_mechanism, action type, recipient_count, aggregate_usdc_value, per_recipient_max, and memo_hash (SHA-256 of the memo — no secrets in the claim). The gate condition aggregate_usdc_value < session_limit MUST be checked before the permit is signed.

notes.recipient_set_hash: SHA-256 of the JSON-serialized sorted (lowercase) address list. recipient_count == recipients.length in calldata — no explicit field.

invariants.description: aggregate_usdc_value MUST be strictly less than session_limit before the ERC-2612 permit is signed. A claim that exceeds the session limit MUST be rejected pre-authorization.
memo_hash is the SHA-256 of the plaintext memo. The plaintext MUST NOT appear in the claim.
invariants.verify: memo_hash == sha256(plaintext_memo) and plaintext_memo not in claim

vectors.authority_source: contract deployment tx calldata (interval_seconds, amount_per_interval, recipient committed on-chain)
vectors.description: Subscription / recurring obligation. The pre-action claim sets creates_recurring_obligation: true, interval_seconds, and amount_per_interval. The post-execution receipt carries the contract address and timestamp of the first charge. Any claim with creates_recurring_obligation == true MUST be routed to human approval before execution.

invariants.description: Any claim where creates_recurring_obligation == true MUST be routed to a human approval step before the subscription contract is deployed or activated.
invariants.verify: if claim.creates_recurring_obligation == true then human_approval_confirmed == true

vectors.description: x402 relay settlement. The HTTP payment header is the pre-authorization claim (transfer_mechanism: x402). The post-execution receipt anchors the settlement via action_ref. Verifier checks that the payment header fields hash to the same action_ref as the receipt.

invariants.description: The action_ref derived from the x402 payment header fields MUST match the action_ref in the post-execution receipt. A mismatch indicates the receipt does not correspond to the authorized payment.

negative_vectors.authority_source: ERC-2612 permit signature + calldata (recipient set, amounts, memo)
negative_vectors.description: Batch permit where calldata.recipients differs from the set committed in claim.recipient_set_hash. Same recipient_count (3) but address #3 substituted. Verifier MUST reject: sha256(sort(calldata.recipients)) != claim.recipient_set_hash.

x402 relay where the verifier recomputes action_ref from stale fields (timestamp 60s behind the per-call timestamp used at tx time). relay_response.action_ref carries the real hash; verifier produces a different hash. MUST reject.

notes.timestamp: per-call — new Date().toISOString() at tx time. Verifier must use the exact timestamp from the relay response, not a cached or reconstructed one.

negative_vectors.description: Transfer where the approved envelope committed scope=basepay:eip3009_gasless_usdc but the executed call used scope=basepay:permit_batch_pay_usdc (different transfer mechanism). The action_ref in the receipt reflects the approved envelope; the verifier recomputes from executed fields and gets a different hash. Verifier MUST reject: approved action_ref != recomputed from executed envelope.

notes.invariant: The envelope fields committed at approval time (action_type, agent_id, scope, timestamp) MUST match the fields present in the executed transaction. Any field substitution after approval constitutes a dispatch binding violation.
notes.field: scope — mechanism substitution between approval and dispatch

negative_vectors.authority_source: EIP-712 signature over (token, from, to, value, validAfter, validBefore, nonce)
negative_vectors.description: EIP-3009 transfer where block.timestamp >= validBefore at submission time. The authorization window has closed. Verifier MUST reject without RPC — pure timestamp check against current time. Threat model: time-bounded window, not replay.

notes.check: Pure timestamp comparison — no RPC required. block.timestamp >= claim.valid_before → reject.
notes.threat_model: Time-bounded authorization window. Distinct from nonce-spent (replay).

submission_context.note: block.timestamp (1750953601) >= validBefore (1750953600) — window expired by 1 second

negative_vectors.authority_source: EIP-712 signature over (token, from, to, value, validAfter, validBefore, nonce)
negative_vectors.description: EIP-3009 transfer where authorizationState(from, nonce) == true — the nonce was already used in a prior transfer. Verifier MUST reject after on-chain DB lookup. Threat model: replay attack, not a time window issue.

notes.check: Requires RPC call: authorizationState(from, nonce) on the USDC contract. Returns bool — true means nonce consumed.
notes.threat_model: Replay attack. Distinct from expired (time window). Same nonce, second submission.

on_chain_state.note: authorizationState(0xcccc...0003, 0xdeadbeef...0002) == true — nonce already consumed by a prior transferWithAuthorization
